intent: a personal dashboard for tracking long-term health experiments

body.intent: a personal dashboard for tracking long-term health experiments

draft.title: a personal dashboard for tracking long-term health experiments
draft.summary: Drafting your prototype from the swipes…
draft.html:
a personal dashboard for tracking long-term health experiments

Building your first draft…

data.intent: a personal dashboard for tracking long-term health experiments

facade.hypothesis: Users prefer warm, encouraging tone over clinical data presentation for health tracking
facade.label: Cheerful check-ins
facade.acceptImplies: Lean into motivational language, celebration of progress, and conversational UI copy
facade.rejectImplies: Adopt clinical terminology, neutral tone, data-first layout without personality

evidence.hypothesis: Users prefer warm, encouraging tone over clinical data presentation for health tracking
evidence.implication: Lean into motivational language, celebration of progress, and conversational UI copy

facade.hypothesis: The user prefers clean, unfussy data visualization over colorful or dense dashboards
facade.label: Minimal line charts
facade.acceptImplies: They want restraint and clarity in how health data appears—thin lines, lots of white space, no decorative elements
facade.rejectImplies: They're drawn to richer visual language—colors, icons, textures, or a more tactile/human feel in their dashboard

facade.hypothesis: Users prefer fluid, animated scrolling with easing over instant jumps when reviewing time-series health data
facade.label: Smooth scrolling
facade.acceptImplies: Build in momentum-based scroll physics and ease-out animations for data review; expect users want to *feel* their data over time
facade.rejectImplies: Favor instant, grid-snapping navigation; users want fast, precise jumps between data points without animation overhead

facade.hypothesis: Users building health dashboards may prefer gentle, low-contrast visual hierarchy over stark contrasts that feel clinical or cold
facade.label: Soft shadows
facade.acceptImplies: Explore warm, diffused lighting; layered depth through subtle gradients; calming visual hierarchy
facade.rejectImplies: Test high contrast, sharp shadows, or flat brightness; clinical clarity may be preferred; consider bolder visual separation

facade.hypothesis: Users building health dashboards want the most important metric (e.g., streak days, weight trend) as a large, glanceable hero—not buried in a table or chart
facade.axisTargeted: Information hierarchy: hero metric vs distributed data
facade.label: Big numbers at top
facade.acceptImplies: User values quick-scan summaries; dashboard should lead with 1–3 key indicators in large type, with details below
facade.rejectImplies: User prefers data distributed equally or wants to avoid cognitive load from hierarchy; might favor flat layouts or detailed breakdowns without emphasis

axes.label: Whether user prefers minimal clinical aesthetics vs. warm, human-centered visual design for health data
Whether user wants data-dense dashboard layout vs. progressive disclosure with one metric at a time
axes.poleA: All at once or step by step?

axes.label: Whether user responds to motivational/encouraging tone vs. neutral, factual reporting voice
Whether user wants celebratory, gamified feedback vs. calm, meditative tracking environment
Whether user prefers raw numbers and charts vs. narrative summaries and insights
Whether user wants instant feedback on actions vs. slow, contemplative animations and transitions

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user prefers minimal clinical aesthetics vs. warm, human-centered visual design for health data
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Iris

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user wants data-dense dashboard layout vs. progressive disclosure with one metric at a time
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Prism

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user responds to motivational/encouraging tone vs. neutral, factual reporting voice
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Lumen

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user wants celebratory, gamified feedback vs. calm, meditative tracking environment
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Aura

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user prefers raw numbers and charts vs. narrative summaries and insights
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Facet

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user wants instant feedback on actions vs. slow, contemplative animations and transitions
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Echo

facade.hypothesis: Users want numbers and metrics displayed prominently over narrative/motivational framing, prioritizing quantitative feedback over emotional validation
facade.label: Data-first clarity
facade.acceptImplies: Design around charts, stats, and objective measurement; minimize celebratory language and motivational copy; treat the dashboard as an instrument panel
facade.rejectImplies: Confirm users prefer warmth and encouragement; lean harder into conversational tone, progress celebration, and human-centered messaging over raw metrics

facade.hypothesis: Users tracking multiple health experiments benefit from persistent sidebar navigation paired with card-based content display for individual experiments
facade.label: Sidebar with cards
facade.acceptImplies: Explore more sidebar-anchored layouts; test how many experiments fit in a sidebar tree before collapsing; refine card sizing and spacing
facade.rejectImplies: Pivot toward tab-based navigation or list-based layouts; question whether persistent navigation is needed; test horizontal scrolling or pagination instead

draft.title: Experiment Tracker Dashboard
draft.summary: A personal health dashboard for monitoring long-term experiments with visual progress tracking, experiment cards, and key metrics at a glance.
